page: Amazon Web Services c5a.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor. It features 8 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.3 GHz. The instance is built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and includes 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a 2.0 GB memory-to-core ratio. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a mixed profile. While Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores fall into the average category, stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU performance tests rank in the poor and weak categories respectively. Memory bandwidth tests show average performance for small, cached blocks, but drop to weak for larger, uncached blocks. Cryptographic and integer operations, supported by hardware-accelerated AES and SHA instructions, perform at average levels.

From a workload perspective, this instance is suited for tasks that benefit from dedicated compute resources but do not require high memory density or local storage. Appropriate workloads include static web serving, software compilation, and basic database operations, where its average performance in Redis SET operations and text processing can be leveraged. However, it is less suitable for memory-intensive applications or large language model inference, as demonstrated by weak performance in medium-sized model benchmarks.
description: Amazon Web Services c5a.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs and 4 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor running at 3.3 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing 2.0 GB per core, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. The instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark results show average performance in Geekbench tests, software compilation, and static web serving, though it exhibits weaker results in stress-ng CPU tests and uncached memory bandwidth. This instance is suited for compute-bound workloads such as web serving, software compilation, and moderate database operations that do not require high memory-to-CPU ratios or local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5a.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs, an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor, and 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory, designed for web serving and software compilation.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c5a.2xlarge is an AMD-powered compute-optimized instance with 8 vCPUs, 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a 2 Gbps network baseline.
tagline: An AMD-powered compute-optimized virtual server with dedicated vCPUs and DDR4 memory for web serving and software compilation workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor with 8 vCPUs and 4 physical cores
Equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory and no local storage
Delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps on the Nitro hypervisor
Average performance in software compilation, static web serving, and database operations
Suited for compute-bound tasks that do not require high memory density
